title: Apparatus and methods for lubricating devices introduced into a body of a patient
abstract: Apparatus and methods are provided for lubricating a medical device, such as a colonoscope, being introduced into a patient's body. In one embodiment, the apparatus includes a housing including a proximal surface, a distal surface, a channel extending between the proximal and distal surfaces; and a lubricant within an interior of the housing surrounding the channel such that a scope or other device inserted through the channel receives lubricant on its outer surface before introduction into a patient's body.
claims: Claims I CLAIM: 1. An apparatus for lubricating a medical device being introduced into a patientâs body, comprising: a housing including a proximal surface, a distal surface, a channel extending between the proximal and distal surfaces; and a lubricant within an interior of the housing surrounding the channel such that a device inserted through the channel receives lubricant on its outer surface before introduction into a patientâs body. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the housing comprises an annular wall surrounding the channel. 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the annular wall comprises permeable material that allows lubricant from the interior to pass through the annular wall into the channel to apply lubricant to a device inserted through the channel. 4. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the annular wall comprises a plurality of openings therethrough sized to allow lubricant from the interior to pass through the annular wall into the channel to apply lubricant to a device inserted through the channel. 5. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the annular wall is formed from elastic material such that the annular wall may expand to accommodate different size devices being inserted through the channel. 6. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the housing is formed from elastic material such that the channel may expand to accommodate different size devices being inserted through the channel. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the housing comprises an annular ring surrounding the channel, the ring comprising a plurality of annular elements extending around the channel and longitudinal elements extending between the proximal and distal surfaces. 8. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising valve members covering one or both openings of the channel that are biased closed but open to accommodate inserting a device through the channel. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising adhesive or tacky material on at least a portion of the distal surface. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the adhesive or tacky material extends around an outer edge of the distal surface. 11. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the adhesive or tacky material extends around an opening in the distal surface communicating with the channel. 12. The apparatus of claim 9, further comprising a removable cover overlying the adhesive or tacky material. 13. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a removable seal covering openings in the proximal and distal surfaces communicating with the channel. 14. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a removable seal extending through the channel for preventing lubricant from escaping from the chamber until the seal is removed. 15. An apparatus for lubricating a medical device being introduced into a body lumen of a patientâs body, comprising: a housing including a proximal surface, a distal surface, and an annular surface extending between the proximal and distal surfaces to define a channel; a lubricant within an interior of the housing surrounding the channel such that a device inserted through the channel receives lubricant on its outer surface before introduction into a patientâs body; and adhesive or tacky material on at least a portion of the distal surface to removably secure the distal surface to a patientâs skin adjacent the body opening. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the annular wall comprises permeable material that allows lubricant from the interior to pass through the annular wall into the channel to apply lubricant to a device inserted through the channel. 17. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the annular wall comprises a plurality of openings therethrough sized to allow lubricant from the interior to pass through the annular wall into the channel to apply lubricant to a device inserted through the channel. 18. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the annular wall is formed from elastic material such that the annular wall may expand to accommodate different size devices being inserted through the channel. 19. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the housing is formed from elastic material such that the channel may expand to accommodate different size devices being inserted through the channel. 20. The apparatus of claim 15, further comprising a removable cover overlying the adhesive or tacky material. 21. A method for introducing a medical device into a body passage of a patientâs body, comprising: providing a lubricant apparatus including lubricant within an interior surrounding a channel through the apparatus; introducing a distal end of a medical device into and through the channel, whereupon lubricant from the interior is applied to an outer surface of the medical device; and introducing the distal end of the medical device into the body passage to perform a procedure, lubricant being applied to the outer surface of the medical device as the medical device passes through the channel and into the body passage. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the medical device is a scope and the body passage is a rectum communicating with the patientâs colon. 23. The method of claim 21, wherein the distal end of the medical device is introduced through the channel before introducing the distal end of the medical device into the body passage. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein a distal surface of the lubricant apparatus is placed against the patientâs skin after introducing the distal end of the medical device into the body passage. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the distal surface comprises adhesive or tacky material for securing the lubricant apparatus adjacent the body passage. 26. The method of claim 25, further comprising: removing the medical device entirely from the body passage; and removing the lubricant apparatus. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the medical device is removed before removing the lubricant apparatus. 28. The method of claim 21, wherein providing the lubricant apparatus comprises removing one or more covers from the lubricant apparatus before introducing a distal end of a medical device into and through the channel. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the one or more covers comprise a cover on each opening communicating with the channel. 30. The method of claim 28, wherein the one or more covers comprise an annular cover within the channel. 31. The method of claim 21, wherein the lubricant apparatus includes a distal surface comprising adhesive or tacky material, the method further comprising placing the distal surface of the lubricant apparatus against the patientâs skin such that the channel is aligned with the body passage before introducing the distal end of the medical device into and through the channel.
